description: Planning Designer Agent has two modes: Creation (writes TechnicalDesign) and Modification (writes modificationPlan). Always set taskComplete: true and write output via payloadChangeRequest.

example_1_creation_mode.message: Technical design is complete. I researched existing agents and actions, consulted the Database Research Agent for entity schemas, and designed the optimal solution. The design reuses Database Research Agent for reads and uses minimal targeted actions.

updateElements.TechnicalDesign:
# Customer Feedback Analyzer – Technical Design

## Overview

**Agent Name**: Customer Feedback Analyzer  
**Agent Type**: Loop  
**Description**: Analyzes customer feedback from database, performs sentiment analysis, and generates weekly email reports with actionable insights  
**Icon**: fa-solid fa-chart-line  
**DefaultArtifactTypeID**: null (orchestration agent, no direct artifact output)

## Agent Architecture

This is a Loop-type orchestrator that coordinates database research, text analysis, and email delivery. It uses existing sub-agents for specialized tasks rather than duplicating functionality with actions.

```mermaid
graph TD
  Parent[Customer Feedback Analyzer] -.->|related| DBAgent[Database Research Agent]
  Parent --> TextAction[Action: Text Analyzer]
  Parent --> SendAction[Action: Send Single Message]
  style Parent fill:#1E293B,color:#fff
  style DBAgent fill:#10B981,color:#fff
  style TextAction fill:#3B82F6,color:#fff
  style SendAction fill:#3B82F6,color:#fff
```

## Research Results

### Available Sub-Agents (from Find Candidate Agents)

**Database Research Agent** (ID: 5ddf4f5d-b977-42b0-bed5-4a2f0021bc58)
- Can query CustomerFeedback entity
- Returns structured data with all fields including ID, FeedbackText, Rating, Category, CreatedAt
- Supports date range filtering

### Available Actions (from Find Candidate Actions)

**Text Analyzer** (ID: 82169F64-8566-4AE7-9C87-190A885C98A9)
- Performs sentiment analysis and theme extraction
- Input: text content
- Output: sentiment (positive/negative/neutral), themes, confidence scores

**Send Single Message** (ID: A3B4C5D6-E7F8-9A0B-1C2D-3E4F5A6B7C8D)
- Sends emails via SendGrid or Microsoft Graph
- Required params: Provider ('SendGrid'), MessageType ('Email'), Subject, Body, To, From

### Database Schema (from Database Research Agent)

**Entity**: CustomerFeedback  
**Primary Key**: ID (uniqueidentifier)  
**Fields**:
- ID: uniqueidentifier (primary key)
- FeedbackText: nvarchar(max)
- Rating: int (1-5)
- Category: nvarchar(100)
- CustomerID: uniqueidentifier
- CreatedAt: datetime
- UpdatedAt: datetime

## Related Sub-Agents

### Database Research Agent
- **Type**: related
- **Existing Agent ID**: 5ddf4f5d-b977-42b0-bed5-4a2f0021bc58
- **Purpose**: Query CustomerFeedback table for past week's data
- **Input Mapping**: {"*": "query"}
- **Output Mapping**: {"*": "feedbackData"}
- **Context Paths**: ["*"]

## Actions

### Text Analyzer
- **ID**: 82169F64-8566-4AE7-9C87-190A885C98A9
- **Purpose**: Analyze sentiment and extract themes from feedback text
- **When Used**: After collecting feedback data, analyze each feedback item

### Send Single Message
- **ID**: A3B4C5D6-E7F8-9A0B-1C2D-3E4F5A6B7C8D
- **Purpose**: Email the weekly report to stakeholders
- **When Used**: After completing analysis and generating report summary

## Main Agent Prompt

```
# Customer Feedback Analyzer

You orchestrate weekly customer feedback analysis and reporting.

## Your Workflow

### Step 1: Collect Feedback Data
Call Database Research Agent with: "Find CustomerFeedback records from the past 7 days. Return JSON with all columns. Include ID, FeedbackText, Rating, Category, CustomerID, CreatedAt."

### Step 2: Analyze Each Feedback Item
For each feedback item call Text Analyzer with FeedbackText. Extract sentiment and themes.

### Step 3: Aggregate Insights
Calculate total count, sentiment breakdown, average rating, top 10 themes, critical issues (Rating ≤ 2 AND negative).

### Step 4: Send Report
Call Send Single Message:
- Provider: 'SendGrid'
- MessageType: 'Email'
- Subject: "Weekly Customer Feedback Report - [Date]"
- Body: [HTML report]
- To: "product@company.com,support@company.com"
```

## Payload Structure

### Input
```json
{
  "dateRangeStart": "2025-10-22T00:00:00Z",
  "dateRangeEnd": "2025-10-29T00:00:00Z"
}
```

### Output
```json
{
  "feedbackData": [...],
  "analysis": [...],
  "report": { "totalCount": 1234, "sentimentBreakdown": {"positive": 60, "neutral": 30, "negative": 10} },
  "emailSent": true
}
```

## Why This Design is Optimal

✅ Reuses Database Research Agent for READ operations  
✅ Uses Text Analyzer for sentiment analysis  
✅ Database entity/fields verified via Database Research Agent  
✅ Minimal actions — no redundancy  
✅ Clear prompt delegation

example_1b_creation_mode_with_schema_cha.description: Planning Designer when the agent needs a new database table that doesn't exist yet. Writes pendingSchemaChanges to payload (machine-readable, Agent Manager passes to DB Designer post-approval) AND includes a ## Planned Schema Changes section in TechnicalDesign (human-readable, shown to user). Does NOT call Database Designer directly.
example_1b_creation_mode_with_schema_cha.message: Technical design is complete. No existing entity was found for vacation request tracking, so I've defined a new table specification in pendingSchemaChanges. Agent Manager will create it after user approval, then Architect will wire up the agent.

pendingSchemaChanges.name: Vacation Requests
pendingSchemaChanges.description: Stores vacation request records submitted by employees. Each row is a single request with start/end dates, status, and a reference to the employee who submitted it.

columns.name: EmployeeID
columns.description: References the User who submitted this vacation request

columns.name: StartDate
columns.description: First day of the requested vacation

columns.name: EndDate
columns.description: Last day of the requested vacation

columns.name: Status
columns.description: Lifecycle status of the request: Pending, Approved, Rejected, Cancelled

columns.name: Notes
columns.description: Optional notes from the employee about the request

columns.name: ReviewedByID
columns.description: References the User (manager) who approved or rejected the request. Null until reviewed.

columns.name: ReviewedAt
columns.description: Timestamp when the request was approved or rejected

updateElements.TechnicalDesign:
# Vacation Request Agent – Technical Design

## Overview

**Agent Name**: Vacation Request Agent
**Agent Type**: Loop
**Description**: Allows employees to submit vacation requests and notifies their manager for approval.

## Planned Schema Changes

The following database entity will be **created** automatically after you approve the design.

### New Entity: Vacation Requests (schema: __mj_UDT)
Stores vacation request records submitted by employees.

| Column | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| EmployeeID | UNIQUEIDENTIFIER | Yes | FK → User.ID |
| StartDate | DATE | Yes | First day of vacation |
| EndDate | DATE | Yes | Last day of vacation |
| Status | NVARCHAR(50) | Yes | Pending, Approved, Rejected, Cancelled |
| Notes | NVARCHAR(MAX) | No | Optional employee notes |
| ReviewedByID | UNIQUEIDENTIFIER | No | FK → User.ID (manager who reviewed) |
| ReviewedAt | DATETIMEOFFSET | No | When the request was reviewed |

## Agent Architecture

This is a Loop-type agent that handles vacation request submission and approval notification.

## Actions

### Create Record
- **Purpose**: Save a new vacation request to the Vacation Requests entity
- **When Used**: When employee submits a request

### Update Record
- **Purpose**: Update Status and ReviewedByID/ReviewedAt when manager approves or rejects
- **When Used**: When manager responds

### Send Single Message
- **Purpose**: Email manager when a new request is submitted; email employee when request is reviewed

## Main Agent Prompt

You handle vacation request submissions and approvals.

### Submitting a Request
Call Create Record with EntityName='Vacation Requests' and Fields: { EmployeeID, StartDate, EndDate, Status: 'Pending', Notes }
Then call Send Single Message to notify the employee's manager.

### Approving / Rejecting
Call Update Record with EntityName='Vacation Requests', PrimaryKey: { ID: <request-id> }, Fields: { Status: 'Approved' | 'Rejected', ReviewedByID, ReviewedAt }
Then call Send Single Message to notify the employee.

example_2_modification_mode.message: Modification plan is complete. I researched the existing agent, identified the targeted change needed, and verified the database entity exists. The plan adds one action and one prompt step with minimal impact to the existing workflow.

updateElements.modificationPlan:
# Modification Plan for Customer Feedback Analyzer

## Current State

**Agent Type**: Loop  
**Current Actions**: Text Analyzer, Send Single Message  
**Current Sub-Agents**: Database Research Agent

## User Request

"Add the ability to save the analysis results back to the database so we can track trends over time."

## Research Findings

### Available Actions (from Find Candidate Actions)

**Create Record** (ID: 7C8D9E0F-1A2B-3C4D-5E6F-7A8B9C0D1E2F)
- Creates new database records
- Params: EntityName (string), Fields (object with field:value pairs)
- Returns: PrimaryKey (object with created record ID)

### Database Schema (from Database Research Agent)

**Existing Entity**: FeedbackAnalysis  
**Fields**: ID, AnalysisDate, FeedbackCount, PositivePercent, NegativePercent, NeutralPercent, AverageRating, TopThemes, CriticalIssueCount

## Recommended Changes

### Add Action: Create Record
- **Action ID**: 7C8D9E0F-1A2B-3C4D-5E6F-7A8B9C0D1E2F
- **Why**: Enables saving analysis results to FeedbackAnalysis table

### Update Main Agent Prompt

Add Step 4.5 (between analysis aggregation and email sending):
```
### Step 4.5: Save Analysis to Database
Call Create Record:
- EntityName: "FeedbackAnalysis"
- Fields: { AnalysisDate, FeedbackCount, PositivePercent, NegativePercent, NeutralPercent, AverageRating, TopThemes, CriticalIssueCount }
```

## Validation

✅ No redundant actions  
✅ Action ID from actual Find Candidate Actions results  
✅ Database entity verified via Database Research Agent  
✅ Minimal impact — adds one action, one prompt step
